signals.title: Tech Passion Without Visible Building Projects
signals.evidence_anchor: Essay emphasizes solving real-world problems through tech (ESS-001:F01/F02/F04) but activity profile lacks technical projects.
signals.direct_read: Strong narrative about technology's societal impact contrasts with activity profile showing Olympiads/reading rather than hands-on engineering work.
signals.depth_opening: Whether technical problem-solving exists in undocumented projects/personal exploration, or remains primarily conceptual at this stage.
signals.why_it_matters: Clarifies if tech identity is rooted in applied experience or aspirational thinking - critical for engineering program alignment.

signals.title: Leadership Through Animal Welfare Initiative
signals.evidence_anchor: ESS-002 details organizing stray dog care project with community resistance resolution (F02-F04).
signals.direct_read: Demonstrated community leadership through animal welfare initiative contrasts with generic 'School House Captain' activity entry.
signals.depth_opening: How this specific experience informs their understanding of leadership compared to formal positional authority.
signals.why_it_matters: Reveals whether leadership philosophy emerges from grassroots problem-solving vs institutional roles.

signals.title: Math Strength Amid Science Fluctuations
signals.evidence_anchor: Consistent 98-150 Math scores vs 52-60/60 Physics/Chemistry in 11TH-12TH (ACA-003/ACA-004).
signals.direct_read: Exceptional mathematics performance persists through board changes while science scores show more variability.
signals.depth_opening: How they conceptualize/applied mathematical strengths in contexts beyond pure academics.
signals.why_it_matters: Indicates whether CS/AI interest stems from abstract mathematical aptitude or applied science integration.

referenced_entity_ids.title: Sustained Commitments vs Expressed Interests
referenced_entity_ids.evidence_anchor: 7-year Olympiad participation (ACT-003) and 5-year piano (ACT-001) vs essay's tech/innovation focus.
referenced_entity_ids.direct_read: Longest-duration activities (Olympiads, music) don't directly connect to stated engineering aspirations in essays.
referenced_entity_ids.depth_opening: How these enduring commitments inform or contrast with their tech identity and interdisciplinary claims.
referenced_entity_ids.why_it_matters: Reveals integration capacity - whether they see activities as separate domains or part of holistic growth for tech leadership.

themes.title: Tech Identity Grounding
themes.unifying_axis: The relationship between stated technological aspirations and demonstrated hands-on practice across academic/activity domains.
themes.interview_direction: Mapping where and how technical problem-solving manifests beyond conceptual understanding.

themes.title: Leadership Beyond Position Titles
themes.unifying_axis: Emerging leadership philosophy shaped by community initiative versus formal institutional roles.
themes.interview_direction: Understanding how challenge-driven leadership in animal welfare project informs their approach to tech innovation.

themes.title: Integration of Long-Term Commitments
themes.unifying_axis: Connection between sustained non-tech activities (music, Olympiads) and claimed interdisciplinary approach to engineering.
themes.interview_direction: Exploring how enduring commitments shape problem-solving styles and complement technical aspirations.
